core_mechanism: sequential conjugate Bayesian updating of a Dirichlet-multinomial (categorical) distribution propagated along a continuously varying parameter, reusing each posterior as the prior at the next step, with a log-Bayes-factor statistic acting as a change-point detector that triggers adaptive resampling when the carried-over prior becomes statistically implausible
brief_reason: The abstract models the probability that a sample falls into each category via a Dirichlet-multinomial model, estimates the next parameter's probabilities by updating that prior with new data, and uses the log Bayes factor as an information-theoretic sensor to detect structural changes and trigger dense resampling.
